name: memory_search
description: Search stored memories using a text query. Performs case-insensitive substring matching on memory content. Returns up to N matching memories sorted by relevance (most recent first). Use this to find past observations, decisions, or context from previous sessions. Do not use this to retrieve a specific memory by known ID—use memory_get instead. Do not use this for complex semantic queries; it does simple keyword matching.

query.description: Search text. Case-insensitive substring match against memory content. Example: 'authentication bug' matches memories containing those words.

type.description: Optional filter to match only memories of a specific type (e.g., 'bugfix', 'decision', 'info'). Case-insensitive.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must be a positive integer. Default is 10.

name: memory_get
description: Retrieve a single memory by its integer ID. Use this when you already know the exact memory ID and need full details (text, type, timestamp, summary). Do not use this for exploratory searches—use memory_search instead.

id.description: Integer ID of the memory to retrieve. Must be a positive integer.

name: memory_delete
description: Delete a memory from the persistent store by its integer ID. Use this to remove unwanted, incorrect, or outdated observations. The deletion is permanent. Returns confirmation of whether the memory was found and deleted.

id.description: Integer ID of the memory to delete. Must be a positive integer.

name: memory_stats
description: Get aggregate statistics about the memory database. Returns total memory count and a breakdown of memory counts by type. Use this to understand the volume and composition of stored memories. Do not use this for health checks—use memory_health instead.

name: memory_health
description: Check if the persistent memory worker service is running and responsive. Returns status and version info. Use this before performing other memory operations if you suspect connectivity issues. Do not use this to query database statistics—use memory_stats instead.

name: memory_timeline
description: Get neighboring memories around a specific memory ID. Returns the target memory plus up to one previous and one next memory in chronological order. Use this to understand the context and sequence of events surrounding a particular memory.

id.description: Integer ID of the memory to get timeline around. Must be a positive integer.
